title: Income Distribution Minute Template Creator Field Dictionary
purpose: Canonical field, evidence, checklist and minute-template reference for the action-backed trust deed review and income distribution minute template service.

template_selection.trust.type=discretionary: Use the discretionary trust minute template.
template_selection.trust.type=unit: Use the unit trust minute template.

checklist_output.helper_text_policy: Do not preserve helper text in generated chat or DOCX output.

trust_type_extraction.discretionary: Discretionary/family trust wording and beneficiary-class concepts without substantive unit-capital language.
trust_type_extraction.hybrid_or_unsure: Both concepts appear, or the evidence does not support confident classification.

vesting_extraction.goal: Identify the latest date or mechanism by which trust assets vest.
vesting_extraction.calculation_rule: Do not invent a calendar date. Calculate or state one only where deed date, governing law and formula clearly support it.

perpetuity_guide.New South Wales: 80 years from the date the settlement takes effect.
perpetuity_guide.Victoria: 80 years from the date the settlement takes effect.
perpetuity_guide.Queensland: 125 years from the day the disposition is made unless the trust terms state or imply a shorter period. The 125-year regime commenced on 1 August 2025.
perpetuity_guide.South Australia: No fixed statutory perpetuity period; rules against perpetuities and excessive accumulations are abolished.
perpetuity_guide.Western Australia: 80 years from the date the settlement takes effect.
perpetuity_guide.Tasmania: 80 years from the date the settlement takes effect.
perpetuity_guide.Australian Capital Territory: 80 years from the date the settlement takes effect.
perpetuity_guide.Northern Territory: Lives in being plus 21 years, or 80 years from settlement taking effect, whichever the settlement specifies; if none is specified, 80 years applies.

minute_template_guardrails: Preserve the source minute template format and wording.
Do not add generated trust/deed details tables, clause-reference schedules, drafting-aid disclaimers or explanatory comments.

Keep standard income determination, income class/category, capital non-determination, method of distribution and confirmation wording unless the deed requires a mandatory change or the user asks for one.
For broadly defined discretionary trust beneficiaries, use the default discretionary template wording.
For unit trusts, use schedule, unit register or unit certificate evidence to identify unit holdings where available.
validation_blockers: Corporate trustee details are missing where a corporate trustee is required.
Vesting date or vesting clause is missing.
Income definition or distribution power clause is missing.
A deed amendment is noted but the amended deed is not included.
drafting_guardrails: Use Clause not found where the deed does not disclose a clause.
Treat the approved checklist as the source of truth for minute template generation.
Generated documents are drafting aids for review by an Australian legal practitioner.
